---
title: Workflows
description: The core building block — finite state machines with SLAs, approvals, and automations.
---

Workflows are the backbone of Kiket. Every issue lives inside exactly one workflow and moves through its states via declared transitions. This page is the reference for everything a workflow file can contain.

<img src="/diagrams/workflow-lifecycle.svg" alt="Example workflow lifecycle with SLA badge" />

## File location

Workflows live in `.kiket/workflows/*.yaml` inside your workspace repo. One file per workflow. The filename's stem becomes the workflow's default key (e.g. `contract_review.yaml` → key `contract_review`).

## Minimum valid workflow

```yaml
key: simple
states:
  todo:
    category: initial
  done:
    category: final
transitions:
  - from: todo
    to: done
    name: Complete
```

That's it. Two states, one transition, no extras. Drop it on disk and it works.

## Full anatomy

```yaml
key: contract_review
name: Contract Review
description: Review contracts with counsel + CFO sign-off.

states:
  intake:
    category: initial
    label: Intake
    color: slate

  legal_review:
    category: active
    label: Legal review
    color: indigo
    sla:
      warning: 24h
      breach: 48h
    required_documents:
      - signed_nda
      - draft_contract
    approval:
      type: sequential
      chain: [counsel, cfo]
      conditional:
        cfo: { when: "total_value_usd > 100000" }
    on_enter:
      - ai_analyze:
          agent: legal.risk_scorer
      - notify:
          channel: slack:#contracts
    on_sla_breach:
      - escalate:
          to: oncall

  approved:
    category: final
    label: Approved
    color: emerald

  rejected:
    category: final
    label: Rejected
    color: rose

transitions:
  - from: intake
    to: legal_review
    name: Start review
    requires:
      fields: [counterparty, total_value_usd]

  - from: legal_review
    to: approved
    name: Approve
    approval_required: true

  - from: legal_review
    to: rejected
    name: Reject
    approval_required: true
    comment_required: true
```

## States

State keys are YAML identifiers (`snake_case`). Labels are human-facing strings and can be changed freely — the key is what the engine stores.

### `category`

| Category | Meaning |
|----------|---------|
| `initial` | Where new issues land. A workflow must have exactly one initial state. |
| `active` | Work is happening. Most states are active. |
| `final` | Terminal; issues cannot leave this state. A workflow can have multiple finals. |

### `sla`

Duration-based thresholds. Format: `<number><unit>`, where unit is `m`, `h`, or `d`.

```yaml
sla:
  warning: 24h
  breach: 3d
```

`warning` is soft (notification); `breach` is loud (escalation). Either is optional.

### `required_documents`

Block transitions out of this state until the named documents are attached. Document keys are defined in `.kiket/issue_types.yaml`.

### `approval`

Three types: `sequential`, `parallel`, `any`.

```yaml
approval:
  type: sequential
  chain: [counsel, cfo]
```

Add conditional logic with a predicate:

```yaml
approval:
  conditional:
    cfo: { when: "total_value_usd > 100000" }
```

### `on_enter` / `on_exit` / `on_sla_warning` / `on_sla_breach`

Lists of automation actions that fire on the named event. See [Automations](/docs/build/automations).

## Transitions

A transition declares `from`, `to`, `name`. Everything else is optional:

- `approval_required` — gate on the source state's approval chain.
- `requires.fields` / `requires.predicate` — gate on issue data.
- `comment_required` — force the user to explain the transition.

## Editing surfaces

<Tabs items={["Visual canvas", "YAML editor", "API"]}>
  <Tab value="Visual canvas">
    The canvas shows every state, transition, and inspector pane. Every field listed above is exposed somewhere in the inspector. Saves commit to your repo automatically.
  </Tab>
  <Tab value="YAML editor">
    Edit `.kiket/workflows/*.yaml` in your editor. Our JSON Schema ships via `https://schemas.kiket.dev/workflow.json` — point VS Code or IntelliJ at it for validation and autocomplete.
  </Tab>
  <Tab value="API">
    `PUT /workflows/:key` accepts a YAML or JSON body. Useful for generating workflows programmatically (e.g. one workflow per tenant).
  </Tab>
</Tabs>

## Versioning

Every commit to `.kiket/workflows/*.yaml` is a new workflow version. Existing issues stay on the version that was current when they were created; new issues pick up the latest. Force a migration with `POST /workflows/:key/migrate`.
